purpose: Defines the !trace {target} slash command — end-to-end trace of an API endpoint, function, type, or data flow across the project from origin to destination. Identifies target type (endpoint/function/type/dataflow) and applies matching strategy (endpoint: frontend call site → route → middleware → handler → database → response; function: definition → call sites → callers; type: definition → consumers → transformations; dataflow: UI component → form handler → API → backend → DB → triggers). Greps for exact term, finds origin definition, walks the chain forward following imports/calls/API requests/event emissions across layer boundaries, notes every transformation/validation/side-effect, flags inconsistencies (type drift, missing error handling, undocumented side effects). Outputs numbered chain with [Layer] tags + Type Flow analysis + Notes section. Read-only investigation.
gotchas: Follow the chain COMPLETELY — don't stop at layer boundaries. The whole point is full-stack tracing.
FLAG inconsistencies: type mismatches, missing error handling, undocumented side effects must be called out.
Target-type-aware strategy: endpoint vs function vs type vs natural-language flow each have different starting points and traversal directions.
Each chain step labels its layer ([Frontend], [Backend], etc.) so reader can see boundary crossings.
patterns: Origin-first traversal: find definition → walk forward.
Layer-tagged chain output for visibility into boundary crossings.
Type Flow as a separate section to surface type drift across layers.
